clinical_note.history_of_present_illness: Acute onset of left eye pain, redness, and blurring of vision after camping trip. Symptoms include photophobia, tearing, and foreign body sensation. Denies visual halos, headaches, or fever. No previous episodes; does not wear contact lenses.

previous_history.family_history: Father with history of high blood pressure; no known family history of eye diseases.

clinical_note.review_of_systems: Reports photophobia, tearing, and foreign body sensation in the affected eye. Denies visual halos, headaches, or fever.
clinical_note.physical_exam: Conjunctival injection and corneal opacity in left eye. Decreased visual acuity in left eye (20/80). Anterior chamber clear. Right eye normal.

assessment.summary: Corneal ulcer in the left eye.

clinical_note.plan: Topical antibiotic drops (Moxifloxacin 0.5%) every 2 hours in affected eye for 24 hours. Corneal scraping for culture. Follow-up in 24-48 hours. Avoid rubbing eye, makeup, contact lenses, swimming pools, and hot tubs. Rest as much as possible. Call if symptoms worsen.
